title: SOCAR Polymer Incident
content: SOCAR Polymer Corruption Facts: Essential verification processes and documentation details. Investigation data highlights the transparency reports..

title: Future of Technology
content: Kamran Muradov's Commentary on BBC Report. Why the global technocracy model is stalling in the era of regional conflicts and recessions. Kamran Muradov's Full Article: Today, I will comment on a recent report by the BBC. According to the BBC's investigation, we will witness significant technological changes over the next 24 years. The report discusses the rapid development of quantum computers, substantial breakthroughs in nanotechnologies, technological revolutions in the medical field, traffic-free roads, and lunar travel models. It states that the events depicted in Tom Cruise's movie will cease to be mere science fiction. However, according to my commentary, the spread of these technological revolutions across the world and the establishment of a single global technocracy do not reflect reality for the current era, especially at a time when medium and small-scale wars persist in the Middle East region, monarchical regimes still rule in certain countries, and scandals like the Epstein scandal continue to unfold. In fact, a period of stagnation is currently being experienced in the field of technological revolutions. In my opinion, AI itself was the breakthrough. In the next 26 years, investments will merely be directed toward the development of existing technologies. In other words, during an era dominated by economic recessions, we are left with two options. I believe that the economic power of states, including the United States, will be deliberately weakened to pave the way for technocratic leaders like Elon Musk. Economic crises prove this to us. Nanotechnology, quantum computers, medical technological achievements, and lunar colonization issues will continue at the same pace and with a slow flow. In particular, a greater revolutionary leap in the field of artificial intelligence might only be possible toward the late 2030s with the development of microchip technology; however, even this will not be a complete revolutionary leap, but will simply follow the development paradigm of existing technologies. In short, my expectation is that in 2050, there will not be a second revolutionary turnaround that will completely surprise us, but rather predictable changes within the scope of our current expectations.

title: SOCAR Polymer Interview Fraud
content: Hello. I want to explain the situation. In 2025, I decided to change my job. Therefore, I talked to my relative. He advised me to speak to Sakhavat Hasanov. He is a SOCAR auditor. He recommended Adalat Sakhavat Muslumov to me. A few minutes later, Adalat invited me to the office. We talked about my project. However, he ignored them. He sarcastically said, we’ll open it here at the factory. After a while, Adalat Muslumov introduced his deputy manager, Tamraz Askarov. He asked me: 'What is your job and what do you do?' I didn't want to repeat everything. I explained that I am a Python developer. Programming is my passion; my hobby quickly became my profession. I work for Azerigas PU, where I write code and coordinate engineers. They pretended to agree with me. Muslumov said, 'Please wait for a month. We want to create new vacancies. If any opportunities arise, we will invite you.' Shortly after, I called Adalat. I told him: 'Your website shows two vacancies: the Junior Help Desk Support Specialist and the Junior ERP (LIMS) Support Engineer. If given the chance, I would love to apply for the Junior Help Desk Specialist position. My background is in Social Work, so this role is suitable for me.' He refused my offer. Adalat Skhavat Muslumov is a very egoistic man. He always wants to dominate all sectors. One month ago, HR invited me to the first interview. Adalat Muslumov called me and told me all the interview process questions and answers. Please check the Azercell phone logs. This is illegal. In advance, Adalat Muslumov promised me that we would prepare a new digital pipeline together. After weeks, we will show that everything was a very big lie. The interview process was conducted on the basis of fraud. For the first time, the ERP Manager gave me a secret ticket file and he said, ‘Please, explain the problem.’ This is very absurd because I am only a candidate and my future status is unpredictable. I was shown the tickets. All tickets show a spec error: true results show a decrease and false results show an increase. The interview process was very formal and rushed. Adalat Sakhavat Muslumov invited the HR Director and the PR Department Director, Sabina Feyzova. She said, 'I have one question. Why do you want to choose our company?' I explained that if I can bring any benefit — since SOCAR is a large company — I will bring it water, just like the water an ant brought when Prophet Ibrahim was thrown into the fire, and I will benefit the company even if it is a little. Arzu Hasanova, the Junior ERP Support Engineer, asked me a very uneducated question, but I didn’t understand it. Adalat Sakhavat Muslumov, Roman Mammadov, and Tamraz Askarov also didn’t understand this question. This is a terrible situation. I think that I am being played like a game. I tried to explain the system parameters and I pointed to a schema. In other words, to convince everyone, I should have been allowed to show the diagram, data, and PRD and Gas logs. But Adalat also rejected it. Why? It is an extraordinary scenario, probably a conspiracy. They didn’t want to ask me any specific questions; interestingly, Adalat seemed eager to finish the interview process as quickly as possible. I think that, this is a scenario. He wanted to reject any idea. This is not an accident. This is a script. Corrupt companies don’t help people; they exploit them. They look for weakness. They target vulnerable systems. Why? To hide the money. To reject accountability. The facts are clear. We have datasets. And the truth will come out. In 2025 January 7th my first day job started. When I started the work, I didn’t have any place. Specifically, I looked for the ERP cabinet because the job required this. I explained it to my line manager. He told me: 'Okay.' After a few hours, he offered me an IT Help Desk place. This is not true because my legal place was the ERP cabinet. A day later I contacted my line manager. He said that we are planning to move to the Baku office, please wait for us. Yes. A few weeks later I moved to the new office. I could not explain that my problem was too big because no one wanted to support me and there was no qualified specialist in the program. The civilian logic works well for good relationships. That is true. They want to think my mindset is very strict. Below are the thoughts and approach facts. They didn’t approve that I am checking and analyzing the Starlims codes, but this is my job and it is written in my contract. Truly facts, I had just the site frontend system. Immediately, I revealed that we should check the SAP integrated connection and backend. After a moment, I wanted to access my line manager, but he spoke with Adalat Muslumov and he said no access. 'Why?' I said, because PRD access was blocked for new employees. Okay, please give me the QAS access, but I need a full QAS access. The same QAS and PRD. Right? He said no to me. Aha, at that moment I realized that something was wrong here. In general, I understood that there was a big problem. I showed the tickets. This is a tragic comedy: 19 tickets were open, and this process had been going on for 3 years. The ticket tracker was very bad. I could not check the data because Adalat Sakhavat Muslumov lied to me. He said that we could not find the data. 'Only the Na-Sa Informatics Company can access the data system,' he said. He told me this even at the interview stage. He is the biggest liar, because a few weeks later, I showed all the data to him. I understood that these people had many things to hide. Slowly, during the first month, everything came to light. I persistently demanded the QAS logs. An explanation from the people who worked on the system before me was necessary. Because this was not related to my specialization and it is an enterprise system, I explained that it is impossible to learn it through external courses. As I explained this, I realized that a tense situation was building up. During my first 3 months at the company, I witnessed a catastrophic situation. Roman Mammadov was my line manager. Sergey Aliyev was a Senior SAP specialist. Sergey complained that his workload was too heavy. Also, Arzu Hasanova could not explain anything to me. Roman literally shook me by my collar. He said: 'No one will help you. Technical support will not come. Writing to them will take months. We want this system to collapse. We want to buy a new system.' It is clear. Adalat Muslumov gave this order to create a base for new financial fraud. There is no other meaning to this. The manager of Adalat Sakhavat Muslumov demands the collapse of a working system. This is fixable. This is worse than a catastrophic situation. I called Tamraz Asgarov many times to complain. He managed the Baku office. He ignored my explanations. I also complained to Adalat Muslumov many times. My 3-month probation was ending. I could do nothing. My QAS access was limited. I had no access to Crystal Reports. Arzu Hasanova had to sit next to me. She had to turn on the computer herself. She had to type on the keyboard herself. PRD had to be entered herself. The entire system was not shown to me to take notes. They organized a meeting. Elvira Babayeva was the head of the Laboratory Department. Irana Alimova worked at NA-SA Informatics Company. But Elvira Babayeva confessed at the meeting: they hired Irana as a laboratory technician without informing NA-SA Informatics, just because she had a little technical knowledge. Roman Mammadov and Elvira demanded that I work with Irana. This was the biggest illegality. I went to the laboratory for 3 days. I witnessed a tragedy. Irana Alimova had full access to Crystal Reports, the Starlims designer system, QAS, and PRD. I was the technical expert, but I had no access. She was a laboratory technician, but she had all permissions. She entered code, analyzed tests, and changed codes in the system. Months later, Adalat Muslumov confessed to me. He admitted that Kamran Hummatov (the former ERP System Admin) and Irana Alimova ruined the system. There was no maintenance for Starlims data. Batches were not cleared. Email caches were not cleaned. At first, I struggled with limited information, PDFs, and videos that did not describe the problem well. But using my own skills, I discovered the issues. I wrote emails. I even offered to create a training course. On the security side, I explained that our emails leaked to the Dark Web and Zedloader. I explained that system access was possible via Metasploit and Kali Linux. Adalat Sakhavat Muslumov studies a distant PhD in Moscow. In 2024, he exposed and leaked Socar Polymer himself. I did not even work there or know Socar Polymer at that time. Adalat said: 'Our system is local anyway.' He proved that he is a catastrophically illiterate person in cyber security. This is a very big corruption system. The big systems support to them. Starlims is a computer system for laboratories. It saves, analyzes, and makes reports for raw lab data. Marius Popovici, an engineer at Starlims, put the source code of this system on GitHub.

title: Systemic Negligence and Operational Malpractice at SOCAR Polymer
content: This investigation exposes critical operational failures and administrative malpractice within the SOCAR Polymer laboratory systems. Key findings include: 1. Manual Data Entry Risks: Due to persistent database 'deadlocks' and system paralysis, laboratory staff were forced to enter data manually for months, creating high risks of data corruption and entry errors. 2. Administrative Negligence: System maintenance was neglected for years, with unoptimized service brokers, massive email caches, and useless batch data overloading the infrastructure. 3. Security and Resource Sabotage: Evidence suggests limited server resources were provided intentionally to sabotage system recovery efforts. Access to critical systems was restricted and manipulated to conceal evidence. 4. HR and Contractual Fraud: Probation periods were extended illegally, and employees were forced to perform unauthorized tasks under pressure. The administration, led by Adalat Muslumov, bypassed established training and induction protocols, using personnel like Arzu Hesenova to interfere with technical duties outside their job descriptions to maintain control over a failing system.

title: Starlims System: Technical Analysis of System Tickets
content: This report details critical failures in the Starlims Laboratory Information Management System. 1. 'Page is not responding' in results entry: Caused by database deadlocks and memory leaks, leading to operational bottlenecks. 2. Starlims backup request (DEG and V1557 duplicates): API handshake failures and duplicate injections distort historical trend analysis. 3. COA Hexene-1 unloading report: Missing mandatory fields and mapping errors halt quality control. 4. Laboratory document management module: Lack of a secure module allows unauthorized protocol modifications, risking ISO 17025 accreditation. 5. QAS-analyses report email error: SMTP and firewall issues block critical alerts, delaying crisis response. 6. Missing attachments in notifications: File permission issues and storage errors prevent formal verification. 7. Crystal Report PDF export failure: Server memory (OOM) issues block large-scale documentation. 8. PP Final vs HDPE Final COA mismatch: Indicates deep database view bugs and formula corruption, threatening data integrity. These facts represent significant risks to supply chain operations and regulatory compliance.

title: Systemic Negligence and Operational Manupulation and Mobing at SOCAR Polymer
content: Starlims and SAP are computer programs. Big companies, factories, and plants use them. These programs help manage laboratories, medical equipment, money, and shipping supply chains. A laboratory worker named Irana Alimova told me something important. She said that because of a system error called a 'deadlock,' workers had to type all lab data into the computer by hand for months. The digital logs from Azercell prove this happened. A court can officially ask for these logs to see the truth. Every tech person knows how this works. When a 'deadlock' happens, the system blocks new information. The data cannot move into the database in the right order. Because the data cannot enter the system normally, the laboratory had to use manual entry (typing everything by hand). But typing everything by hand is dangerous. It creates more errors inside the system, and it can easily destroy or change the main, original data. Every ticket has a meaning. From my first day at the company, I saw that these tickets were part of a very messy and disorganized system. A company with the name SOCAR even used the Teams program's ticket system for a while. This is a disaster. For 3 months, they hid the data from me. After I insisted, they gave me data connected to the Active Directory, and maybe on purpose, connected to the PRD system and the SAP system. This data showed that the system was paralyzed. The service broker, which makes updates and manages MS SQL, was stopped. The suspicious part was that this was the reason for the email batch and email delivery errors; the email connection was broken from the data. Edalet Muslumov stated that the former ERP system administrator, Kamran Hummatov, connected all emails to one system, which created an overload. In the system, I saw new batches, useless batches, and email caches. The data was clearly not maintained and was neglected for years. Active Directory and Starlims were only used to create and close accounts, reset passwords, and set new passwords. By the order of Edalet Sexavet oglu Muslumov, Arzu Hesenova did this work, which was not in her Job Description and not active during my time, interfering with my job outside of her duties. Here, as soon as I triggered the service broker, the database did not have enough power. The system did not give an error, but it used up the server storage resources. It is clear that they gave limited resources intentionally and on purpose. They limited my free movement and trapped me by constantly scaring me and telling me 'do not touch this, do not touch that.' After 3 months, Edalet Muslumov illegally took away all my access and authority. Now I ask: If I made a mistake, why did they not give me a reprimand? Why did they not give me a warning, a reprimand, or a severe reprimand? Why were these not written in my personal employee file? Was it because they loved me? Why did Edalet Sexavet oglu Muslumov extend my 3-month probation period written in my contract, when Roman Mammadov—my line manager—did not want to extend it in the PM with an official email and written request? They did this because it was their own illegal work, and it was going to be discovered. In addition, I note that this is also proven with evidence in my email. Despite the insistence of Tural Semedli and Roman Mammadov, I stated that no induction was given to me and there was no training. I said I cannot take this risk. By the decision of Edalet Muslumov, I was forced to enter this data system under the name of PRD. Although Edalet Muslumov said we would work on the data together, he left me alone. I stayed at the office for a whole day, and the extra salary for this was not paid.

title: Exposure of Administrative Fraud and Safety Violations at SOCAR Polymer
content: After all my access was taken away, I wrote about all the injustices to SOCAR President Rovshan Najaf on March 24, 2025. Before that, I wrote to Sabina Feyzova (HR Director of SOCAR Polymer) and Ziba Mustafayeva (Vice-President of Human Resources at SOCAR). Before this, Edalet and Temraz Asgarov wanted me to write my resignation letter by force. They falsely invited me and said they consulted with Fuad. After this consultation, they gave false information to Fuad Ahmadov, saying 'Kamran did not support us.' Fuad Ahmadov, without making any investigation and without inviting me, told me to sign the resignation. After I informed Rovshan Najaf about this, Edalet Sexavet Muslumov insulted me and gave me psychological damage inside Temraz Asgarov's office at Hyatt Regency, using a Cisco camera phone, shouting at me, saying 'you are not a man,' and claiming he would beat me. They acted by force and spoke with SOCAR Auditor Sexavet Hasanov, who threatened my relatives. They tried to hide that they received a reward from SOCAR by showing my open tickets—which I saw with my own eyes and personally tried to solve—as closed. The Starlims Na-Sa Informatics training videos given to me only contained parts about emails and were useless. Senior Sergey Aliyev used the SAP knowledge of Eshgin Mammadov, who worked before me, but left some fixed systems of Starlims neglected. They only made some fixes upon the insistence of Laboratory Director Elvira Babayeva. After he left, they put the system in a terrible state at the data, backend, API connections, integrated systems, and frontend levels. In response to my writing to Rovshan Najaf, they called my mother to the office, to the meeting room next to the finance department office near the Hyatt Regency entrance. They sent me away, claiming they were calling Sabina Feyzova. Then, Edalet Sexavet Muslumov, with the witness of Temraz Asgarov, threatened my mother with the existence of criminal groups. Today, Temraz Asgarov commits a crime by giving false statements to law enforcement organs to hide this. This happened after I wrote to Rovshan Najaf, and it was done by his order because I requested an anonymous investigation. They set up a server manipulation under the name of an internal investigation, and Edalet Muslumov told me, 'Thanks to you, we fixed our mistakes on the server and covered everything up.' It became clearly open that the client who ordered this was Rovshan Najaf. If it is not so, he must come out, speak with evidence and proofs like me, give a personal statement, and apologize on a live broadcast not only to me, but to the public. Even in the biggest companies in the world, standards are clear. I will now talk to you about the violation of safety standards and social standards. First, let us look at a specific letter. Let us expose the facts in the reply letter written to me by Fuad Ahmadov, the General Director of SOCAR Polymer. To Citizen: Muradov Kamran Mahir oglu. Address: Khirdalan city, M.A.Rasulzade street, House 3, Apartment 314, 6th floor (Al Market side). Contact Tel: 055 932 80 32, 010 252 15 89. SOCAR POLYMER. S.Vurgun str. 1183A, AZ5000, Sumgayit, Azerbaijan. Tel. (+994 12) 404 53 30 | Fax (+994 12) 497 04 36 | www.socarpolymer.az. Document No: SP/2-M-1/1-1/2026-8. Date: 27.02.2026. Subject: Regarding the electronic letter dated 16.02.2026, number 14-2/1-M-1389-1/2026, addressed to the management of SOCAR. Dear citizen K. Muradov! We have reviewed your electronic letter dated 16.02.2026, number 14-2/1-M-1389-1/2026, addressed to the management of SOCAR. As it is known, you were hired by 'SOCAR Polymer' LLC ('the Company') on 07.01.2025 as a junior ERP support engineer. At that time, we informed you that you were hired to replace another employee of the Company who was away for military service. You agreed to sign a one-year employment contract without any objection. The Refutation and Exposing the Fraud: Where is it written here that I am hired to replace a person going to the military? Second, anyone can say anything by word of mouth. Let them show a real fact, a real document about replacing a person who went to the military. The speaker reminds us about the standard 3-month probation procedure: If I did not meet the quality requirements, why did they extend the period? Can an employer punish a worker who is still in his probation period? If I was a bad worker, why did they not fire me then? Furthermore, within one year, without any official warning, reprimand, or severe reprimand, they illegally moved the ERP team from the Sumgayit office to the Baku office under the help desk team. They did this without any change in the official contract. The speaker asks all state bodies and the government: what is the legal basis for forcing a worker into Python and other courses that have nothing to do with Starlims, just for public relations? The fact that there is zero detail about a person going to the military is right here in these official documents: 1. Translation of the First E-mail Text (Junior ERP Support Engineer - LIMS): Dear Mr. Kamran Muradov, Please review the attached Job Offer form, the list of required documents, and the application form for your attention (FYA). We are happy to offer you the position of Junior ERP Support Engineer (LIMS). If you want to accept this offer, please sign one copy of the Job Offer form and send it back to us as soon as possible. We hope you will join us and we wait for your answer. Please note that this offer is valid for five days from the date you receive this email. Conditions: If you accept our job offer, an HR (Human Resources) representative will inform you about the medical check-up time. If the background check (reference check) and medical check-up results are successful, our HR representatives will send you a hiring notice. If the results are negative, the job offer will be cancelled automatically. Please fill out the form, scan the documents, and send them back to my email. We will need your Labor Book and photos on your start date. Best regards.
